headline: Street protest organisers face RM20,000 fines under amended PAA
context: The 2024 amendment to the Peaceful Assembly Act increased fines for organisers of assemblies deemed non-compliant from RM10,000 to RM20,000.

cards.big: PAA 2024 amendment doubles fines to RM20,000 for non-compliant assemblies
cards.sub: Increased penalties deter exercise of constitutional assembly rights

cards.big: PAA 2012 Section 9 requirement of 10-day notice retained with higher penalties
cards.sub: Courts have upheld conviction of organisers for spontaneous peaceful gatherings

cards.big: UN Special Rapporteur on assembly rights criticised PAA in 2023 country visit
cards.sub: Notification requirements should facilitate, not restrict, peaceful assemblies

cards.big: Number of registered assemblies dropped 38% in 2024 compared to 2023
cards.sub: Higher penalties correlate with reduced exercise of fundamental freedoms

cards.big: Doubling fines for peaceful assembly is doubling down on restricting rights
Gabungan Bertindak Malaysia organised largest post-amendment rally in January 2025
